002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.microservice.jetty;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.ReflectionUtils.*;
020import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.IoUtils.*;
021import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ObjectUtils.*;
022import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.Shorts.*;
023import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.StringUtils.*;
024import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.ThrowableUtils.*;
025import static org.apache.juneau.marshall.collections.JsonMap.*;
026
027import java.io.*;
028import java.net.*;
029import java.time.*;
030import java.util.*;
031import java.util.concurrent.atomic.*;
032import java.util.logging.*;
033
034import org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.*;
035import org.apache.juneau.commons.reflect.*;
036import org.apache.juneau.config.event.*;
037import org.apache.juneau.marshall.cp.*;
038import org.apache.juneau.microservice.*;
039import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
040import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.auth.*;
041import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.health.*;
042import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
043import org.eclipse.jetty.ee11.servlet.*;
044import org.eclipse.jetty.server.*;
045
046import jakarta.servlet.*;
047
048/**
049 * Drives the Jetty server lifecycle on behalf of the microservice via the {@link MicroserviceListener} fan-out.
050 *
051 * <p>
052 * Contributed by {@link JettyConfiguration} as a <c>@Bean</c>.  The microservice's {@link Microservice#start()}
053 * call invokes {@link #onStart(Microservice)}, which:
054 * <ol>
055 *    <li>Picks the first available port from the resolved {@link JettySettings#getPorts() ports} (falling back to
056 *       <c>Jetty/port</c> config, then <c>Jetty-Port</c> manifest, then <c>8000</c>).
057 *    <li>Resolves the Jetty {@link Server} — preferring an externally-supplied <c>@Bean Server</c>, otherwise
058 *       building one from <c>jetty.xml</c> via the {@link JettyServerFactory}.
059 *    <li>Mounts servlets from {@code Jetty/servlets}, {@code Jetty/servletMap}, {@code Jetty/servletAttributes}
060 *       config sections, plus auto-mounts every {@code @Bean Servlet} whose runtime class is annotated with
061 *       {@link Rest @Rest} (at {@link Rest#path()}, or <c>"/"</c> when no path is set).
062 *    <li>Starts the server and publishes the bound port via the {@code availablePort} / {@code juneau.serverPort}
063 *       system properties.
064 * </ol>
065 *
066 * <p>
067 * {@link #onStop(Microservice)} performs the inverse: stops the server gracefully in a worker thread.
068 *
069 * <p>
070 * External callers reach this component via
071 * {@code Microservice.getInstance().getBeanStore().getBean(JettyServerComponent.class).orElseThrow()}.
072 *
073 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
074 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauMicroserviceJetty">juneau-microservice-jetty Basics</a>
075 * </ul>
076 *
077 * @since 10.0.0
078 */
079@SuppressWarnings({
080   "java:S115", // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., KEY_SERVLET_CONTEXT_HANDLER)
081   "resource" // ms.getBeanStore() is owned by the microservice lifecycle; do not close here.
082})
083public class JettyServerComponent implements MicroserviceListener {
084
085   private static final String KEY_SERVLET_CONTEXT_HANDLER = "ServletContextHandler";
086
087   @SuppressWarnings({
088      "java:S2245" // Used only to pick a benign ephemeral listen port (non security-sensitive); SecureRandom is unnecessary.
089   })
090   private static final Random RANDOM = new Random();
091
092   /** Default bounded graceful-shutdown drain timeout applied to the Jetty server when none is configured. */
093   static final Duration DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT = Duration.ofSeconds(30);
094
095   private final Messages messages = Messages.of(JettyServerComponent.class);
096   private final AtomicReference<Server> server = new AtomicReference<>();
097   private final AtomicReference<Microservice> microservice = new AtomicReference<>();
098   private final AtomicReference<Duration> shutdownSettleDelay = new AtomicReference<>(Duration.ZERO);
099
100   /**
101    * Env-driven sentinel for {@code availablePort}; {@link Optional#empty()} when unset (in which case
102    * {@link #onStart(Microservice)} publishes the bound port back as the {@code availablePort} system property).
103    */
104   @Value("${availablePort}")
105   Optional<String> availablePortEnv = oe();
106
107   /**
108    * Env-driven sentinel for {@code juneau.serverPort}; {@link Optional#empty()} when unset (in which case
109    * {@link #onStart(Microservice)} publishes the bound port back as the {@code juneau.serverPort} system property).
110    */
111   @Value("${juneau.serverPort}")
112   Optional<String> serverPortEnv = oe();
113
114   private static int[] parseIntArray(String csv) {
115      if (ie(csv))
116         return new int[0];
117      var parts = csv.split(",");
118      var out = new int[parts.length];
119      for (var i = 0; i < parts.length; i++)
120         out[i] = Integer.parseInt(parts[i].trim());
121      return out;
122   }
123
124   private static int findOpenPort(int[] ports) {
125      for (var port : ports) {
126         if (port == 0)
127            port = RANDOM.nextInt(32767 - ports[0] + 1) + ports[0];
128         try (var ss = new ServerSocket(port)) {
129            return port;
130         } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") IOException e) {
131            // Port is in use, try next port in array
132         }
133      }
134      return 0;
135   }
136
137   private static String normalizePathSpec(String rawPath) {
138      var p = rawPath == null ? "" : rawPath;
139      if (p.isEmpty() || "/".equals(p))
140         return "/*";
141      if (! p.startsWith("/"))
142         p = "/" + p;
143      if (! p.endsWith("/*"))
144         p = trimTrailingSlashes(p) + "/*";
145      return p;
146   }
147
148   private static String normalizeExactPathSpec(String rawPath) {
149      var p = rawPath == null ? "" : trimTrailingSlashes(rawPath);
150      if (p.isEmpty())
151         return "/";
152      if (! p.startsWith("/"))
153         p = "/" + p;
154      return p;
155   }
156
157   /**
158    * Resolves the top-level Jetty mount path-specs for an auto-discovered {@code @Rest}-annotated servlet.
159    *
160    * <p>
161    * Delegates to {@link RestContext#resolveTopLevelPaths(Class, Object, BeanStore)} so the runtime-override
162    * resolution chain (programmatic &gt; {@code getPaths()} getter &gt; annotation default) is honored at
163    * mount time. The programmatic Builder rung is N/A for Jetty auto-discovery (the {@code RestContext} does
164    * not exist yet at this point in the lifecycle); users can substitute by overriding {@code getPaths()} on
165    * the resource class, or by leaning on SVL inside {@code @Rest(paths=...)} elements (e.g.
166    * {@code paths={"$C{health.paths}"}}) so each element is resolved through {@code Config} /
167    * {@code $E{...}} / {@code $S{...}} before being comma-split.
168    *
169    * @return The normalized exact-match path-specs (for multi-mount), or the singular {@code @Rest(path)}-style
170    *    {@code "/...&#47;*"} pathspec when no {@code paths} are resolved.
171    */
172   private static String[] restPathsFor(Servlet servlet, BeanStore store) {
173      var cls = servlet.getClass();
174      var r = cls.getAnnotation(Rest.class);
175      if (r == null)
176         return new String[] {"/*"};
177      var resolved = RestContext.resolveTopLevelPaths(cls, servlet, store);
178      if (resolved.length > 0)
179         return Arrays.stream(resolved).map(JettyServerComponent::normalizeExactPathSpec).toArray(String[]::new);
180      return new String[] {normalizePathSpec(r.path())};
181   }
182
183   /**
184    * Constructor.
185    */
186   @SuppressWarnings({
187      "java:S1186" // Empty constructor; all state is initialized via onStart(Microservice) when wired through the bean store.
188   })
189   public JettyServerComponent() {}
190
191   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
192   @SuppressWarnings({
193      "java:S3776", // Cognitive complexity acceptable for server creation logic
194      "java:S1141", // Nested try blocks scope checked-exception translation tightly to single call sites
195      "java:S6541"  // Brain Method acceptable for server startup wiring logic
196   })
197   public void onStart(Microservice ms) {
198      try {
199         microservice.set(ms);
200         var store = ms.getBeanStore();
201         ClassInfo.of(this).inject(this, store);
202         var cf = ms.getConfig();
203         var mf = ms.getManifest();
204         var vr = ms.getVarResolver();
205         var settings = store.getBean(JettySettings.class).orElseGet(() -> JettySettings.create().build());
206
207         // Port resolution: settings > Jetty/port config > Jetty-Port manifest > 8000
208         var ports = settings.getPorts();
209         if (ports == null)
210            ports = cf.get("Jetty/port").as(int[].class)
211               .orElseGet(() -> mf.get("Jetty-Port").map(JettyServerComponent::parseIntArray).orElseGet(() -> ints(8000)));
212         var availablePort = findOpenPort(ports);
213
214         if (availablePortEnv.isEmpty())
215            System.setProperty("availablePort", String.valueOf(availablePort));
216
217         // Prefer a @Bean-supplied Server, else build one from jetty.xml.
218         var injectedServer = store.getBean(Server.class).orElse(null);
219         if (nn(injectedServer)) {
220            server.set(injectedServer);
221         } else {
222            var factory = store.getBean(JettyServerFactory.class).orElseGet(BasicJettyServerFactory::new);
223            var jettyXml = settings.getJettyXml();
224            var jettyConfig = cf.get("Jetty/config").orElseGet(() -> mf.get("Jetty-Config").orElse("jetty.xml"));
225            var resolveVars = coalesce(settings.getJettyXmlResolveVars(), cf.get("Jetty/resolveVars").asBoolean().orElse(false));
226            boolean resolveVars2 = isTrue(resolveVars);
227
228            if (jettyXml == null)
229               jettyXml = loadSystemResourceAsString("jetty.xml", ".", "files");
230            if (jettyXml == null)
231               throw rex("jetty.xml file ''{0}'' was not found on the file system or classpath.", jettyConfig);
232
233            if (resolveVars2)
234               jettyXml = vr.resolve(jettyXml);
235
236            ms.getLogger().info(jettyXml);
237
238            try {
239               server.set(factory.create(jettyXml));
240            } catch (Exception e) {
241               throw toRex(e);
242            }
243         }
244
245         // Publish back to the store for downstream beans / lookups.
246         store.addBean(Server.class, server.get());
247
248         // Graceful-shutdown wiring (zero-downtime k8s rollouts):
249         //  - Apply a bounded stopTimeout so server.stop() drains in-flight requests before the connector closes.
250         //    Precedence: JettySettings > Jetty/stopTimeout config > jetty.xml value (if any) > 30s default.
251         //  - Remember the settle delay so onStop() can let the LB observe the /readyz 503 before draining.
252         //  - Mark readiness ready so a freshly-(re)started service serves traffic.
253         var stopTimeoutOverride = settings.getStopTimeout() != null
254            ? settings.getStopTimeout()
255            : cf.get("Jetty/stopTimeout").asLong().map(Duration::ofMillis).orElse(null);
256         if (stopTimeoutOverride != null)
257            server.get().setStopTimeout(Math.max(0L, stopTimeoutOverride.toMillis()));
258         else if (server.get().getStopTimeout() <= 0L)
259            server.get().setStopTimeout(DEFAULT_STOP_TIMEOUT.toMillis());
260         shutdownSettleDelay.set(coalesce(settings.getShutdownSettleDelay(), cf.get("Jetty/shutdownSettleDelay").asLong().map(Duration::ofMillis).orElse(Duration.ZERO)));
261         ReadinessState.resolve(store).markReady();
262
263         // Track each servlet pathSpec with its declaring source so we can fail loudly on collisions.
264         var mountedPaths = new LinkedHashMap<String,String>();
265
266         for (var s : cf.get("Jetty/servlets").asStringArray().orElse(new String[0])) {
267            try {
268               var c = info(Class.forName(s));
269               if (c.isAssignableTo(RestServlet.class)) {
270                  var rs = (RestServlet)c.newInstance();
271                  mountWithCollisionCheck(rs, rs.getPath(), "Jetty/servlets[" + s + "]", mountedPaths);
272               } else {
273                  throw rex("Invalid servlet specified in Jetty/servlets.  Must be a subclass of RestServlet: {0}", s);
274               }
275            } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {
276               throw toRex(e);
277            }
278         }
279
280         cf.get("Jetty/servletMap").asMap().orElse(EMPTY_MAP).forEach((k, v) -> {
281            try {
282               var c = info(Class.forName(v.toString()));
283               if (c.isAssignableTo(Servlet.class)) {
284                  var rs = (Servlet)c.newInstance();
285                  mountWithCollisionCheck(rs, k, "Jetty/servletMap[" + k + "]", mountedPaths);
286               } else {
287                  throw rex("Invalid servlet specified in Jetty/servletMap.  Must be a subclass of Servlet: {0}", cn(v));
288               }
289            } catch (ClassNotFoundException e) {
290               throw toRex(e);
291            }
292         });
293
294         cf.get("Jetty/servletAttributes").asMap().orElse(EMPTY_MAP).forEach(this::addServletAttribute);
295
296         // Auto-mount @Bean AuthFilterChain at /* before any servlet registration so the filter
297         // runs before routing for all requests.
298         store.getBean(AuthFilterChain.class).ifPresent(chain -> addFilter(chain, "/*"));
299
300         // Auto-discover @Rest servlets contributed via @Configuration/@Bean methods.
301         // Path source precedence: @Rest(path=...) on the resource class, else "/".
302         for (var e : store.getBeansOfType(Servlet.class).entrySet()) {
303            var servlet = e.getValue();
304            var cls = servlet.getClass();
305            if (cls.getAnnotation(Rest.class) == null)
306               continue;
307            var pathSpecs = restPathsFor(servlet, store);
308            var source = "@Bean " + cls.getName() + (ine(e.getKey()) ? "[" + e.getKey() + "]" : "");
309            for (var pathSpec : pathSpecs)
310               checkPathCollision(pathSpec, source, mountedPaths);
311            addServlet(servlet, pathSpecs);
312         }
313
314         if (serverPortEnv.isEmpty())
315            System.setProperty("juneau.serverPort", String.valueOf(availablePort));
316
317         server.get().start();
318         ms.out(messages, "ServerStarted", getPort());
319      } catch (Exception e) {
320         throw toRex(e);
321      }
322   }
323
324   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
325   public void onStop(Microservice ms) {
326      final Logger logger = ms.getLogger();
327      // Flip readiness out of service BEFORE the connector stops so /readyz returns 503 and the load balancer /
328      // Kubernetes stops routing new traffic while in-flight requests drain.  /livez stays healthy so the pod is
329      // not killed mid-drain.  The settle delay (default 0) gives the LB a window to observe the 503.
330      ReadinessState.resolve(ms.getBeanStore()).markOutOfService();
331      ms.out(messages, "DrainingRequests");
332      sleepQuietly(shutdownSettleDelay.get());
333      var t = new Thread("JettyServerComponentStop") {
334         @Override /* Overridden from Thread */
335         public void run() {
336            try {
337               var s = server.get();
338               if (s == null || s.isStopping() || s.isStopped())
339                  return;
340               ms.out(messages, "StoppingServer");
341               s.stop();
342               ms.out(messages, "ServerStopped");
343            } catch (Exception e) {
344               logger.log(Level.WARNING, localizedMessage(e), e);
345            }
346         }
347      };
348      t.start();
349      try {
350         t.join();
351      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") InterruptedException e) {
352         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
353      }
354   }
355
356   private static void sleepQuietly(Duration d) {
357      if (d == null || d.isZero() || d.isNegative())
358         return;
359      try {
360         Thread.sleep(d.toMillis());
361      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") InterruptedException e) {
362         Thread.currentThread().interrupt();
363      }
364   }
365
366   @Override /* Overridden from MicroserviceListener */
367   public void onConfigChange(Microservice ms, ConfigEvents events) {
368      // No-op: Intentional empty implementation - this component is purely lifecycle-driven.
369   }
370
371   /**
372    * Adds an arbitrary servlet to this Jetty server.
373    *
374    * @param servlet The servlet instance.
375    * @param pathSpec The context path of the servlet.
376    * @return This object.
377    */
378   @SuppressWarnings({
379      "java:S3878" // Array wrapper required to dispatch to varargs overload; without it the call recurses infinitely back to this single-String overload.
380   })
381   public JettyServerComponent addServlet(Servlet servlet, String pathSpec) {
382      if (nn(pathSpec) && ! pathSpec.endsWith("/*"))
383         pathSpec = trimTrailingSlashes(pathSpec) + "/*";
384      return addServlet(servlet, new String[]{pathSpec});
385   }
386
387   /**
388    * Adds an arbitrary servlet to this Jetty server at one or more context paths.
389    *
390    * @param servlet The servlet instance.
391    * @param pathSpecs The context paths of the servlet.
392    * @return This object.
393    */
394   public JettyServerComponent addServlet(Servlet servlet, String...pathSpecs) {
395      var sh = new ServletHolder(servlet);
396      for (var pathSpec : pathSpecs)
397         getServletContextHandler().addServlet(sh, pathSpec);
398      return this;
399   }
400
401   /**
402    * Adds a servlet filter to this Jetty server at the specified URL pattern.
403    *
404    * @param filter The filter instance.
405    * @param urlPattern The URL pattern the filter applies to (e.g. {@code "/*"}, {@code "/api/*"}).
406    * @return This object.
407    */
408   @SuppressWarnings({
409      "java:S3878" // Array wrapper required to dispatch to varargs overload; without it the call recurses infinitely back to this single-String overload.
410   })
411   public JettyServerComponent addFilter(jakarta.servlet.Filter filter, String urlPattern) {
412      return addFilter(filter, new String[]{urlPattern});
413   }
414
415   /**
416    * Adds a servlet filter to this Jetty server at one or more URL patterns.
417    *
418    * @param filter The filter instance.
419    * @param urlPatterns The URL patterns the filter applies to.
420    * @return This object.
421    */
422   public JettyServerComponent addFilter(jakarta.servlet.Filter filter, String... urlPatterns) {
423      var fh = new FilterHolder(filter);
424      for (var urlPattern : urlPatterns)
425         getServletContextHandler().addFilter(fh, urlPattern, EnumSet.of(DispatcherType.REQUEST));
426      return this;
427   }
428
429   /**
430    * Adds an attribute to the Jetty server.
431    *
432    * @param name The server attribute name.
433    * @param value The attribute value.
434    * @return This object.
435    */
436   public JettyServerComponent addServletAttribute(String name, Object value) {
437      getServer().setAttribute(name, value);
438      return this;
439   }
440
441   /**
442    * Returns the context path that this server is using.
443    *
444    * @return The context path.
445    */
446   public String getContextPath() {
447      return getServletContextHandler().getContextPath();
448   }
449
450   /**
451    * Returns the hostname of this microservice.
452    *
453    * @return The hostname.
454    */
455   public String getHostName() {
456      String hostname = "localhost";
457      try {
458         hostname = InetAddress.getLocalHost().getHostName();
459      } catch (@SuppressWarnings("unused") UnknownHostException e) {
460         // Cannot determine hostname, use default "localhost"
461      }
462      return hostname;
463   }
464
465   /**
466    * Returns the port that this server is listening on.
467    *
468    * @return The port.
469    */
470   public int getPort() {
471      for (var c : getServer().getConnectors())
472         if (c instanceof ServerConnector c2)
473            return c2.getPort();
474      throw new IllegalStateException("Could not locate ServerConnector in Jetty server.");
475   }
476
477   /**
478    * Returns whether this server is using <js>"http"</js> or <js>"https"</js>.
479    *
480    * @return The protocol.
481    */
482   public String getProtocol() {
483      for (var c : getServer().getConnectors())
484         if (c instanceof ServerConnector c2)
485            for (var cf : c2.getConnectionFactories())
486               if (cf instanceof SslConnectionFactory)
487                  return "https";
488      return "http";
489   }
490
491   /**
492    * Returns the underlying Jetty server.
493    *
494    * @return The underlying Jetty server.
495    * @throws IllegalStateException If the server has not been created yet.
496    */
497   public Server getServer() {
498      return Objects.requireNonNull(server.get(), "Server not found.  Microservice has not been started yet.");
499   }
500
501   /**
502    * Finds and returns the servlet context handler defined in the Jetty container.
503    *
504    * @return The servlet context handler.
505    * @throws IllegalStateException If the context handler is not defined.
506    */
507   public ServletContextHandler getServletContextHandler() {
508      var obj = getServer().getAttribute(KEY_SERVLET_CONTEXT_HANDLER);
509      if (obj instanceof ServletContextHandler obj2)
510         return obj2;
511      throw new IllegalStateException("Servlet context handler not found in jetty server or at attribute '" + KEY_SERVLET_CONTEXT_HANDLER + "'");
512   }
513
514   /**
515    * Returns the URI where this server is listening.
516    *
517    * @return The URI.
518    */
519   public URI getURI() {
520      var cp = getContextPath();
521      try {
522         return new URI(getProtocol(), null, getHostName(), getPort(), "/".equals(cp) ? null : cp, null, null);
523      } catch (URISyntaxException e) {
524         throw toRex(e);
525      }
526   }
527
528   private void mountWithCollisionCheck(Servlet servlet, String rawPath, String source, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
529      var pathSpec = normalizePathSpec(rawPath);
530      checkPathCollision(pathSpec, source, mountedPaths);
531      addServlet(servlet, pathSpec);
532   }
533
534   private void checkPathCollision(String pathSpec, String source, Map<String,String> mountedPaths) {
535      var prior = mountedPaths.get(pathSpec);
536      if (nn(prior))
537         throw rex("Servlet mount path collision: ''{0}'' is already mounted by {1}; refused by {2}.", pathSpec, prior, source);
538      mountedPaths.put(pathSpec, source);
539   }
540}
